ambiguities.description: What entities, relationships, and boundaries define this system?
How do users and processes interact with the system over time?
What rules, constraints, and compliance requirements apply?
What external systems, integrations, and deployment constraints exist?
What defines MVP, done, and out-of-scope for this construction?
Channels (email, SMS, push, in-app, webhook, chat)
Message types and templates (transactional, promotional, alert, digest)
Audience and recipient model (user, segment, broadcast, dynamic)
Consent and preferences (opt-in, opt-out, channel preferences, frequency)
Retries and failure handling (dead letter, backoff, manual retry)
Scheduling and throttling (immediate, scheduled, rate-limited, batch)
Personalization and dynamic content (variables, localization, A/B)
Audit and logging (send log, open/click tracking, retention)
Provider integrations (SMTP, Twilio, Firebase, OneSignal, custom)
Compliance and opt-out rules (CAN-SPAM, GDPR, TCPA)
Analytics and deliverability (bounce rates, spam scores, engagement)
MVP boundary: which channels, message types, and guarantees are in scope

questions.question: What does success look like for this system?
Who are the primary users and what is the organization size?
What is the timeline and what constraints (budget, compliance, team) apply?
What channels are required? (email, SMS, push, in-app, webhook, chat)
What message types are in scope? (transactional, promotional, alert, digest)
What delivery guarantee is required? (at-most-once, at-least-once, exactly-once)
How is consent managed? (explicit opt-in, implicit, granular per-channel)
Is scheduling or throttling required? (immediate only, scheduled, rate-limited)
What compliance rules apply? (CAN-SPAM, GDPR, TCPA, none)
Are open/click tracking or engagement analytics required?

assumptions.assumption: Messages are sent to known users or segments with valid contact information
No single channel is assumed as primary unless explicitly specified
Transactional messages have higher delivery priority than promotional
Compliance requirements must be explicitly declared

suggested_closures.decision: Notifications and messaging construction will support multi-channel delivery with explicit delivery guarantees and consent management.
suggested_closures.rationale: Channel, guarantee, and consent choices determine the architecture, provider selection, and compliance surface.

seed_tasks.title: Define channels and message type taxonomy
seed_tasks.transformation: Document supported channels and message categories.
seed_tasks.evidence_requirement: Channel and message taxonomy is documented.
seed_tasks.review_predicate: Any message can be classified by channel and type.

seed_tasks.title: Define delivery guarantee and retry strategy
seed_tasks.transformation: Document delivery semantics, retry policy, and dead-letter handling.
seed_tasks.evidence_requirement: Delivery model is documented with failure handling.
seed_tasks.review_predicate: A developer can implement retry and dead-letter logic from the document.

seed_tasks.title: Define consent and preference model
seed_tasks.transformation: Document opt-in, opt-out, and channel preference rules.
seed_tasks.evidence_requirement: Consent model is documented with user flows.
seed_tasks.review_predicate: A user can manage preferences without contacting support.

seed_tasks.title: Define compliance and audit requirements
seed_tasks.transformation: Document applicable regulations and audit retention rules.
seed_tasks.evidence_requirement: Compliance checklist and audit log requirements are documented.
seed_tasks.review_predicate: An auditor can verify compliance from logs and documentation.

residuals.description: System scope and success criteria are not yet defined by the principal.
Constraints, compliance requirements, and non-goals have not been documented.
Required channels and message types are not defined.
Consent and preference model is not documented.
